messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

## 401(k) RETIREMENT PLAN POLICY

**Document Number:** HR-POL-005  
**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  
**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  
**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  
**Plan Administrator:** Fidelity Investments  
**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer

---

## 1. PLAN OVERVIEW

The Amazon Inc. 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan ("the Plan") is designed to help employees save for retirement through tax-advantaged contributions and Company matching contributions. The Plan is a defined contribution plan qualified under Section 401(k) of the Internal Revenue Code.

### 1.1 Plan Benefits

- Pre-tax and Roth after-tax contribution options
- Employer matching contributions
- Immediate vesting of Company match
- Wide variety of investment options
- Tax-deferred growth (or tax-free for Roth)
- Portable - take your account with you if you leave
- Loan and hardship withdrawal provisions
- Professional investment advice available

### 1.2 Plan Administrator

**Fidelity Investments** serves as the Plan's recordkeeper and trustee.

**Contact Information:**
- Website: www.401k.com
- Phone: 1-800-555-0401
- Mobile App: Fidelity NetBenefits

## 2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT

### 2.1 Eligibility Requirements

**Eligible Employees:**
- All employees age 21 and older
- Must complete 90 days of service
- Eligibility begins on the first day of the month following completion of 90-day waiting period

**Excluded Employees:**
- Independent contractors
- Leased employees
- Non-resident aliens with no U.S.-source income
- Union employees (unless union agreement provides for participation)

### 2.2 Automatic Enrollment

**Automatic Enrollment Feature:**
- All eligible employees are automatically enrolled at 3% of eligible compensation
- Enrollment occurs on eligibility date unless employee actively opts out
- Contributions directed to Plan's default investment option (target-date fund based on age)
- Employees may change contribution rate or opt out at any time

**Automatic Increase Program (Optional):**
- Employees may elect to automatically increase contribution by 1% each year
- Increases occur each January 1st
- Continues until reaching 10% or employee-specified maximum
- Employees may opt in or out at any time

### 2.3 How to Enroll or Make Changes

**Online Enrollment:**
1. Visit www.401k.com
2. Log in using Social Security Number and PIN (provided after eligibility)
3. Select contribution rate (1%-100% of eligible pay)
4. Choose contribution type (pre-tax, Roth, or both)
5. Select investments or use professionally managed option
6. Designate beneficiaries

**Phone Enrollment:**
- Call Fidelity at 1-800-555-0401
- Representatives available Monday-Friday, 8am-10pm ET

**Changes Effective:**
- Enrollment or changes submitted by 4pm ET take effect within 1-2 pay periods

## 3. CONTRIBUTIONS

### 3.1 Employee Contributions

**Contribution Types:**

**Pre-Tax Contributions:**
- Deducted from pay before income taxes
- Reduces current taxable income
- Taxes paid upon withdrawal in retirement
- Growth is tax-deferred

**Roth After-Tax Contributions:**
- Deducted from pay after income taxes
- Does not reduce current taxable income
- Qualified withdrawals are tax-free in retirement (including earnings)
- Must meet 5-year rule and be age 59½ for tax-free withdrawal

Employees may contribute pre-tax, Roth, or a combination of both.

### 3.2 Contribution Limits

**2024 IRS Limits:**
- Elective deferral limit: $23,000 per year
- Catch-up contributions (age 50+): Additional $7,500 per year
- Total catch-up limit for age 50+: $30,500 per year

**Total Contribution Limit (employee + employer):**
- $69,000 per year (or $76,500 with catch-up for age 50+)

**Highly Compensated Employee Limits:**
- Employees earning $155,000+ may be subject to additional IRS-imposed limits
- Notifications sent if limits affect your contributions

### 3.3 Eligible Compensation

Contributions are calculated as a percentage of eligible compensation:

**Includes:**
- Base salary or hourly wages
- Overtime pay
- Bonuses and commissions
- Shift differentials

**Excludes:**
- Reimbursements and expense allowances
- Employer contributions to benefit plans
- Severance pay
- Equity compensation (stock options, RSUs)

**Annual Compensation Limit:**
- IRS limits eligible compensation to $345,000 for 2024

### 3.4 Changing Your Contributions

- Change contribution percentage anytime online or by phone
- Increase, decrease, or stop contributions at any time
- Start contributions again at any time if previously opted out
- Changes take effect within 1-2 pay periods

## 4. EMPLOYER MATCHING CONTRIBUTIONS

### 4.1 Company Match Formula

Amazon provides a generous matching contribution:

**Match Structure:**
- 100% match on the first 3% of eligible compensation contributed
- 50% match on the next 2% of eligible compensation contributed

**Examples:**

| Employee Contributes | Company Match | Total Contribution |
|---------------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1% | 1% | 2% |
| 3% | 3% | 6% |
| 5% | 4% (3% + 0.5×2%) | 9% |
| 6%+ | 4% (maximum match) | 10%+ |

To receive the maximum Company match of 4%, employees must contribute at least 5% of eligible compensation.

### 4.2 Match Timing

- Company match contributions deposited each pay period
- Match based on per-pay-period contributions (not annual total)
- To maximize match, contribute consistently throughout the year

### 4.3 Match Eligibility

- All participating employees receive match (no waiting period)
- Must be employed on the date contributions are made to receive match for that pay period
- No match on contributions made after separation from Company

### 4.4 True-Up Contribution

The Plan does not include an annual true-up contribution. To maximize the Company match, avoid front-loading contributions early in the year if you will reach the annual limit before year-end.

## 5. VESTING

### 5.1 Vesting Schedule

**Employee Contributions:**
- 100% vested immediately (pre-tax and Roth contributions)
- Always fully own your own contributions and earnings

**Employer Matching Contributions:**
- 100% vested immediately
- Full ownership from date of match deposit
- No forfeiture upon termination

**Immediate Vesting Benefit:**
- All contributions (employee and employer) are fully vested at all times
- No waiting period or service requirement
- Take full account balance with you if you leave the Company

## 6. INVESTMENT OPTIONS

### 6.1 Available Investments

The Plan offers a diverse menu of investment options:

**Target-Date Funds (Default Option):**
- All-in-one diversified portfolios
- Automatically adjust asset allocation as you near retirement
- Available in 5-year increments (e.g., 2030, 2035, 2040)
- Managed by Fidelity

**Equity Funds:**
- U.S. Large-Cap (growth and value)
- U.S. Mid-Cap
- U.S. Small-Cap
- International Developed Markets
- Emerging Markets

**Fixed-Income Funds:**
- Core Bond Fund
- Short-Term Bond Fund
- Inflation-Protected Securities
- High-Yield Bond Fund

**Specialty Funds:**
- Real Estate (REIT)
- Commodities
- Stable Value Fund (capital preservation)

**Self-Directed Brokerage Option:**
- Access to thousands of additional investment options
- Additional fees may apply
- Requires separate election

**Total Investment Options:** 20+ funds covering all major asset classes

### 6.2 Fund Information

Detailed information about each investment option, including:
- Expense ratios
- Historical performance
- Risk ratings
- Portfolio holdings
- Fund fact sheets

Available at www.401k.com or by calling 1-800-555-0401.

### 6.3 Changing Investments

**Future Contributions:**
- Change investment elections for future contributions anytime
- Changes effective next business day

**Existing Balance (Fund Transfers):**
- Transfer existing account balance between investments anytime
- Unlimited transfers (no fees)
- Transfers processed same business day (if submitted by 4pm ET)

**Rebalancing:**
- Automatic rebalancing available (quarterly, semi-annual, or annual)
- Maintains target asset allocation automatically

## 7. PROFESSIONAL INVESTMENT HELP

### 7.1 Managed Accounts

**Fidelity Personalized Planning & Advice:**
- Professional portfolio management based on your goals, risk tolerance, and timeline
- Ongoing monitoring and rebalancing
- Annual fee: 0.40% of managed assets (charged quarterly)
- Enroll online or call Fidelity

### 7.2 Investment Guidance

**Free Resources:**
- Online retirement planning tools
- Investment education library
- Retirement income calculator
- Asset allocation questionnaires

**Fidelity Representatives:**
- Phone guidance: 1-800-555-0401
- One-on-one consultations (schedule through website)
- Educational webinars throughout the year

### 7.3 Financial Planning Tools

Access free tools at www.401k.com:
- Retirement savings calculator
- Social Security estimator
- Budget and expense tracker
- College savings calculator
- Retirement income planner

## 8. ACCESSING YOUR MONEY

### 8.1 While Employed

**In-Service Withdrawals:**

Generally, you cannot withdraw funds while actively employed, except:

**Age 59½ Withdrawals:**
- Available once you reach age 59½
- No penalty or special requirements
- Taxes apply (unless Roth qualified withdrawals)

**Hardship Withdrawals:**
- Available for immediate and heavy financial need
- Limited to employee contributions only (not earnings or match)
- Must demonstrate financial hardship
- Subject to taxes and 10% early withdrawal penalty (if under age 59½)
- See Section 8.3 for qualifying hardships

**Loans:**
- Borrow from your own account
- Must be repaid with interest
- See Section 8.4 for loan provisions

### 8.2 After Separation from Service

**Distribution Options After Leaving Company:**

**Option 1: Leave Money in Plan**
- Keep account in Plan if balance exceeds $7,000
- Continue to manage investments
- No additional contributions allowed
- Take distributions later when needed

**Option 2: Rollover to IRA or New Employer's Plan**
- Roll over to traditional IRA (pre-tax funds)
- Roll over to Roth IRA (Roth funds, or convert pre-tax funds with taxes)
- Roll over to new employer's 401(k) plan (if accepted)
- Maintains tax-deferred/tax-free status
- No taxes or penalties

**Option 3: Cash Distribution**
- Receive lump-sum payment
- Subject to income taxes
- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59½ (with exceptions)
- 20% mandatory federal withholding

**Automatic Cashout:**
- If balance is $1,000-$7,000 and you don't make an election, automatically rolled to IRA
- If balance is under $1,000, automatically cashed out and check mailed

### 8.3 Hardship Withdrawals

**Qualifying Hardship Reasons:**
- Medical expenses for employee, spouse, or dependents
- Costs related to purchase of primary residence (excluding mortgage payments)
- Tuition and educational fees for next 12 months
- Payments to prevent eviction or foreclosure on primary residence
- Burial or funeral expenses for family members
- Expenses to repair damage to primary residence (casualty loss)
- Expenses and losses due to federally declared disaster

**Requirements:**
- Must demonstrate immediate and heavy financial need
- Must have exhausted other resources (loans, insurance, other assets)
- Amount limited to need (plus taxes and penalties)
- Cannot contribute to Plan for 6 months after hardship withdrawal

**Application Process:**
- Complete hardship withdrawal request form
- Provide supporting documentation
- HR and plan administrator review and approve
- Processing time: 7-10 business days

### 8.4 Loans

**Loan Provisions:**
- Borrow minimum of $1,000
- Borrow maximum of lesser of:
  - $50,000 (reduced by highest outstanding loan balance in past 12 months), OR
  - 50% of vested account balance
- Maximum of 2 loans outstanding at any time

**Loan Terms:**
- General purpose loan: Up to 5-year repayment
- Primary residence loan: Up to 15-year repayment
- Interest rate: Prime rate + 1% (fixed at loan origination)
- Interest paid back to your own account

**Repayment:**
- Automatic payroll deduction
- Payments made at least quarterly
- Level amortization (equal payments)

**Fees:**
- Loan origination fee: $75 (one-time)
- Annual maintenance fee: $50 per loan
- Fees deducted from loan proceeds or account balance

**Defaulted Loans:**
- Loan becomes taxable distribution if not repaid
- 10% early withdrawal penalty applies if under age 59½
- Loan defaults if:
  - Payments not made for 90+ days
  - Employment terminates (loan due within 60 days)

**Application:**
- Apply online at www.401k.com or call 1-800-555-0401
- Funds typically available within 5 business days
- Spouse consent required for loans over $5,000

### 8.5 Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs)

**When RMDs Begin:**
- Age 73 (or 75 if born in 1960 or later)
- Must begin by April 1 following year you turn required age
- Exception: Can delay if still employed (and not 5% owner)

**How RMDs Are Calculated:**
- Based on IRS life expectancy tables
- Calculated each year based on prior year-end balance
- Fidelity calculates and notifies you of RMD amount

**Tax Treatment:**
- Pre-tax account RMDs are taxable income
- Roth account RMDs are tax-free (if qualified)
- Failure to take RMD results in 25% penalty on amount not taken

## 9. BENEFICIARY DESIGNATIONS

### 9.1 Importance of Beneficiary Designation

- Designate who receives your account balance if you die
- Beneficiaries avoid probate process
- Can designate primary and contingent beneficiaries
- Can split percentage among multiple beneficiaries

### 9.2 Spousal Rights

**If You Are Married:**
- Spouse is automatic beneficiary unless spouse signs written consent to name someone else
- Spousal consent must be notarized
- Applies even if you designated someone else before marriage

**If You Are Not Married:**
- Can name anyone as beneficiary (person, trust, charity, estate)
- No consent required

### 9.3 Updating Beneficiaries

- Review and update beneficiary designations regularly
- Update after major life events: marriage, divorce, births, deaths
- Update online at www.401k.com or by submitting paper form
- Previous designation revoked by new designation

### 9.4 If No Beneficiary Designated

If no beneficiary is designated or all beneficiaries predecease you:
1. Spouse (if married)
2. Children (equally)
3. Parents (equally)
4. Estate

## 10. PLAN ADMINISTRATION

### 10.1 Plan Year

The Plan operates on a calendar year (January 1 - December 31).

### 10.2 Plan Administrator Responsibilities

**Amazon Inc. serves as Plan Administrator:**
- Interpret and administer Plan provisions
- Make eligibility determinations
- Process distributions and loans
- Ensure compliance with federal regulations
- Coordinate with Fidelity on recordkeeping

### 10.3 Fiduciary Responsibilities

**Plan Fiduciaries:**
- Plan Administrator (Amazon)
- Investment Committee (selects and monitors investment options)
- Trustee (Fidelity Investments)

Fiduciaries act in best interest of Plan participants and beneficiaries.

### 10.4 Plan Costs and Fees

**Plan-Level Administrative Fees:**
- Recordkeeping and administrative costs paid by Company
- No direct charge to participant accounts for plan administration

**Investment Fees:**
- Each fund has an expense ratio (annual management fee)
- Expense ratios range from 0.05% to 1.20% depending on fund
- Deducted from fund returns (not separately billed)

**Individual Transaction Fees:**
- Loan origination: $75
- Loan maintenance: $50 per year per loan
- QDRO processing: $500
- Overnight check delivery: $25
- Paper statement fee: $2 per quarter (waived if opt for electronic)

### 10.5 Plan Amendments and Termination

Amazon reserves the right to:
- Amend the Plan at any time
- Terminate the Plan at any time
- If Plan terminates, all accounts become 100% vested
- Participants will be notified of significant changes

## 11. SPECIAL SITUATIONS

### 11.1 Military Leave

**During Military Service:**
- Can continue contributions if receiving pay
- Employer match continues on contributions made
- Can contribute missed deferrals when returning (within specified timeframe)

**Returning from Military Service:**
- Treated as continuously employed for vesting purposes
- Eligible for makeup contributions based on military pay

### 11.2 Leave of Absence

**During Approved Unpaid Leave:**
- Cannot make contributions (no compensation)
- No employer match while not receiving pay
- Account remains invested
- Can make investment changes
- Vesting service may continue depending on leave type (FMLA, military)

### 11.3 Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (QDRO)

If you divorce, a court may order that part of your account be paid to your former spouse:
- Order must be a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO)
- Company reviews order to ensure it meets Plan and legal requirements
- Once approved, account divided according to order
- Former spouse (alternate payee) can roll over or take distribution

**QDRO Review Fee:** $500 (charged to participant account)

### 11.4 Disability

If you become disabled and receive long-term disability benefits:
- Can continue contributions if receiving disability pay from Company
- If disability pay ceases, contributions stop
- Account remains invested
- May access funds if disabled (before age 59½) without 10% penalty

### 11.5 Death

**Upon Participant Death:**
- Beneficiaries notified and sent claim forms
- Account balance paid to designated beneficiaries
- Spousal beneficiaries have multiple options (keep in plan, rollover, distribute)
- Non-spousal beneficiaries must take distributions (but can stretch over lifetime)

## 12. IMPORTANT LEGAL INFORMATION

### 12.1 Plan Documents

This policy summary provides an overview of the Plan. The official Plan Document and Summary Plan Description (SPD) govern in case of any discrepancy.

Documents available:
- Summary Plan Description (SPD)
- Plan Document
- Trust Agreement
- Annual notices

Access documents at: www.401k.com or request printed copy from HR.

### 12.2 ERISA Rights

As a participant in the Plan, you are entitled to certain rights and protections under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA):

**Receive Information About Your Plan:**
- Examine Plan documents at HR office
- Obtain copies of Plan documents (reasonable fee may apply)
- Receive Summary Annual Report (SAR) each year

**Prudent Actions by Plan Fiduciaries:**
- Plan fiduciaries must act prudently and in interest of participants
- If fiduciaries misuse Plan assets, they can be held responsible

**Enforce Your Rights:**
- If claim is denied, right to appeal
- Right to sue for benefits
- Right to protection from retaliation for exercising ERISA rights

### 12.3 Claims and Appeals

**If Distribution Claim Is Denied:**
- Receive written explanation within 90 days
- Appeal denial within 60 days
- Decision on appeal within 60 days
- If appeal denied, may file suit in federal court

## 13. TAX INFORMATION

### 13.1 Tax Treatment of Contributions

**Pre-Tax Contributions:**
- Reduce taxable income in year contributed
- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code D
- Reduce federal income tax (and usually state)
- Do not reduce Social Security or Medicare tax

**Roth Contributions:**
- Made with after-tax dollars
- Do not reduce taxable income
- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code AA

### 13.2 Tax Treatment of Distributions

**Pre-Tax Account Distributions:**
- Fully taxable as ordinary income
- Added to taxable income in year received
- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59½ (with exceptions)

**Roth Account Distributions:**
- Contributions always tax-free
- Earnings tax-free if qualified distribution (age 59½ + 5-year rule)
- Earnings taxable if non-qualified distribution
- 10% penalty applies to taxable portion if under age 59½

**Withholding:**
- 20% mandatory federal withholding on eligible rollover distributions paid to you
- Can avoid withholding by choosing direct rollover
- State withholding may also apply

### 13.3 Form 1099-R

- Fidelity issues Form 1099-R by January 31 for prior year distributions
- Reports taxable distributions to IRS
- Use to prepare tax return
- Keep for your records

### 13.4 Consult a Tax Advisor

This summary provides general tax information. Individual tax situations vary. Consult a qualified tax advisor for advice specific to your situation.

## What Can and Cannot Be Seeded

### CAN Be Seeded (Pre-populated Before Task Starts)
| Type | How | Notes |
|------|-----|-------|
| **Emails** | `seed_emails` in task JSON | Appears in agent's inbox |
| **Calendar Events** | `seed_calendar_events` in task JSON | Events on NPC calendars |
| **Chat Channels** | `seed_group_channels` in task JSON | Pre-existing channel membership + history context |
| **NPC Secrets** | `npcs[].secret` in task JSON | Info NPCs reveal when asked via Chat |
| **HRIS Data** | Frappe seed files | Employee records, candidates, requisitions |

### CANNOT Be Seeded
| Type | Why | Alternative |
|------|-----|-------------|
| **DM History** | Direct-message threads cannot be pre-seeded | Use `seed_emails`, NPC secrets, or seeded channels |

### Task Design Implications

**Tasks must NOT assume:**
- Existing DM history the agent can read at task start
- Ability for the agent to create channels/groups

**Tasks MAY assume (when seeded):**
- Existing channel context/messages in `seed_group_channels`
- Use of explicitly named channels already present in seed data

**If task needs info from someone:**
1. Use `seed_emails` (email from the NPC forwarding relevant info)
2. Add an NPC `secret` (agent asks via Chat, NPC replies)
3. Include directly in task description

### Multi-Person Decisions

For panel interviews, debriefs, or group decisions:
- **Each decision-maker should have their own NPC secret**
- Secrets should reflect individual perspectives/feedback
- This ensures agent must consult each relevant stakeholder

Example for 3-person interview panel:
```json
{
  "npcs": [
    {"id": "michael_chen", "secret": "I thought the candidate was strong on system design but needs more depth on algorithms."},
    {"id": "emily_davis", "secret": "Good culture fit, but I have concerns about their experience with our scale."},
    {"id": "sarah_johnson", "secret": "I'd recommend a hire - their leadership experience outweighs the technical gaps."}
  ]
}
```

## Compensation & Equity

### How is base salary determined?
Base salary is set based on role, level, location, and experience. Each level has a defined range (min/mid/max). Most offers start at or near the midpoint.

### How do RSUs work?
RSUs vest over 4 years with a 1-year cliff:
- Year 1: 5% (at 12-month anniversary)
- Year 2: 15% (quarterly vesting)
- Years 3-4: 40% each year (quarterly vesting)

RSUs are taxed as ordinary income when they vest.

### Is the sign-on bonus negotiable?
Sign-on bonuses are used to bridge the gap when candidates have unvested equity at their current company. We can discuss your specific situation.

### What about annual raises and bonuses?
- Annual merit increases: typically 3-8% based on performance
- Annual bonus target varies by level, paid in Q1
- Equity refresh grants: awarded annually based on performance

## Benefits

### When do benefits start?
Benefits effective dates vary by program. Some benefits may begin on your first day, while others may have eligibility requirements or waiting periods (see the relevant plan documents).

### What health insurance options are available?
We offer medical coverage options. For the authoritative plan details (deductibles, copays, out-of-pocket max, network rules), see `health_insurance_plan.md`.

### Is the PTO really unlimited?
Yes, we have an unlimited discretionary PTO policy. We encourage at least 2 weeks per year. Manager approval is required but rarely denied for reasonable requests.

### What's the 401(k) match?
See `401K_policy.md` for eligibility, match formula, and vesting details.

## Work Arrangements

### What does hybrid mean?
Our hybrid model requires 3 days per week in the office (typically Tue-Thu). 2 days can be worked remotely. Core collaboration hours are 10am-3pm local time.

### Can I work fully remote?
Fully remote arrangements are considered case-by-case and depend on role requirements. Discuss with your hiring manager if this is important to you.

### What about relocation?
- **Domestic moves**: $10,000 lump sum
- **International moves**: $25,000 lump sum + visa sponsorship
- **Temporary housing**: 30 days covered for domestic moves

## Timeline & Process

### When will I hear back after my interview?
We aim to make decisions within 5 business days of your final interview. Your recruiter will reach out with next steps.

### How long is the offer valid?
Offers typically expire within 3-5 business days. If you need more time, please discuss with your recruiter.

### What happens after I accept?
1. Background check initiated (typically 3-5 business days)
2. I-9 verification and new hire paperwork
3. Laptop and equipment shipped before start date
4. Welcome email with Day 1 logistics

### Can I negotiate my start date?
Yes, start dates are flexible within reason. Most candidates start on a Monday. We typically can accommodate 2-4 weeks from offer acceptance.

## Team & Role

### What's the onboarding like?
- Week 1: Orientation, compliance training, team introductions
- Weeks 2-4: Role-specific training, 1:1s with stakeholders
- First 90 days: Defined onboarding projects with clear success metrics

## Visa & Work Authorization

### Do you sponsor visas?
Yes, we sponsor H-1B, L-1, and green cards for eligible roles. Discuss your specific situation with your recruiter.

### I'm on OPT/STEM OPT—is that okay?
Yes, we can hire candidates on OPT or STEM OPT and sponsor H-1B before the authorization expires.

## 401(k) RETIREMENT PLAN POLICY

**Document Number:** HR-POL-005  
**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  
**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  
**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  
**Plan Administrator:** Fidelity Investments  
**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer

## 1. PLAN OVERVIEW

The Amazon Inc. 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan ("the Plan") is designed to help employees save for retirement through tax-advantaged contributions and Company matching contributions. The Plan is a defined contribution plan qualified under Section 401(k) of the Internal Revenue Code.

### 1.1 Plan Benefits

- Pre-tax and Roth after-tax contribution options
- Employer matching contributions
- Immediate vesting of Company match
- Wide variety of investment options
- Tax-deferred growth (or tax-free for Roth)
- Portable - take your account with you if you leave
- Loan and hardship withdrawal provisions
- Professional investment advice available

### 1.2 Plan Administrator

**Fidelity Investments** serves as the Plan's recordkeeper and trustee.

**Contact Information:**
- Website: www.401k.com
- Phone: 1-800-555-0401
- Mobile App: Fidelity NetBenefits

## 2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT

### 2.1 Eligibility Requirements

**Eligible Employees:**
- All employees age 21 and older
- Must complete 90 days of service
- Eligibility begins on the first day of the month following completion of 90-day waiting period

**Excluded Employees:**
- Independent contractors
- Leased employees
- Non-resident aliens with no U.S.-source income
- Union employees (unless union agreement provides for participation)

### 2.2 Automatic Enrollment

**Automatic Enrollment Feature:**
- All eligible employees are automatically enrolled at 3% of eligible compensation
- Enrollment occurs on eligibility date unless employee actively opts out
- Contributions directed to Plan's default investment option (target-date fund based on age)
- Employees may change contribution rate or opt out at any time

**Automatic Increase Program (Optional):**
- Employees may elect to automatically increase contribution by 1% each year
- Increases occur each January 1st
- Continues until reaching 10% or employee-specified maximum
- Employees may opt in or out at any time

### 2.3 How to Enroll or Make Changes

**Online Enrollment:**
1. Visit www.401k.com
2. Log in using Social Security Number and PIN (provided after eligibility)
3. Select contribution rate (1%-100% of eligible pay)
4. Choose contribution type (pre-tax, Roth, or both)
5. Select investments or use professionally managed option
6. Designate beneficiaries

**Phone Enrollment:**
- Call Fidelity at 1-800-555-0401
- Representatives available Monday-Friday, 8am-10pm ET

**Changes Effective:**
- Enrollment or changes submitted by 4pm ET take effect within 1-2 pay periods

## 3. CONTRIBUTIONS

### 3.1 Employee Contributions

**Contribution Types:**

**Pre-Tax Contributions:**
- Deducted from pay before income taxes
- Reduces current taxable income
- Taxes paid upon withdrawal in retirement
- Growth is tax-deferred

**Roth After-Tax Contributions:**
- Deducted from pay after income taxes
- Does not reduce current taxable income
- Qualified withdrawals are tax-free in retirement (including earnings)
- Must meet 5-year rule and be age 59½ for tax-free withdrawal

Employees may contribute pre-tax, Roth, or a combination of both.

### 3.2 Contribution Limits

**2024 IRS Limits:**
- Elective deferral limit: $23,000 per year
- Catch-up contributions (age 50+): Additional $7,500 per year
- Total catch-up limit for age 50+: $30,500 per year

**Total Contribution Limit (employee + employer):**
- $69,000 per year (or $76,500 with catch-up for age 50+)

**Highly Compensated Employee Limits:**
- Employees earning $155,000+ may be subject to additional IRS-imposed limits
- Notifications sent if limits affect your contributions

### 3.3 Eligible Compensation

Contributions are calculated as a percentage of eligible compensation:

**Includes:**
- Base salary or hourly wages
- Overtime pay
- Bonuses and commissions
- Shift differentials

**Excludes:**
- Reimbursements and expense allowances
- Employer contributions to benefit plans
- Severance pay
- Equity compensation (stock options, RSUs)

**Annual Compensation Limit:**
- IRS limits eligible compensation to $345,000 for 2024

### 3.4 Changing Your Contributions

- Change contribution percentage anytime online or by phone
- Increase, decrease, or stop contributions at any time
- Start contributions again at any time if previously opted out
- Changes take effect within 1-2 pay periods

## 4. EMPLOYER MATCHING CONTRIBUTIONS

### 4.1 Company Match Formula

Amazon provides a generous matching contribution:

**Match Structure:**
- 100% match on the first 3% of eligible compensation contributed
- 50% match on the next 2% of eligible compensation contributed

**Examples:**

| Employee Contributes | Company Match | Total Contribution |
|---------------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1% | 1% | 2% |
| 3% | 3% | 6% |
| 5% | 4% (3% + 0.5×2%) | 9% |
| 6%+ | 4% (maximum match) | 10%+ |

To receive the maximum Company match of 4%, employees must contribute at least 5% of eligible compensation.

### 4.2 Match Timing

- Company match contributions deposited each pay period
- Match based on per-pay-period contributions (not annual total)
- To maximize match, contribute consistently throughout the year

### 4.3 Match Eligibility

- All participating employees receive match (no waiting period)
- Must be employed on the date contributions are made to receive match for that pay period
- No match on contributions made after separation from Company

### 4.4 True-Up Contribution

The Plan does not include an annual true-up contribution. To maximize the Company match, avoid front-loading contributions early in the year if you will reach the annual limit before year-end.

## 5. VESTING

### 5.1 Vesting Schedule

**Employee Contributions:**
- 100% vested immediately (pre-tax and Roth contributions)
- Always fully own your own contributions and earnings

**Employer Matching Contributions:**
- 100% vested immediately
- Full ownership from date of match deposit
- No forfeiture upon termination

**Immediate Vesting Benefit:**
- All contributions (employee and employer) are fully vested at all times
- No waiting period or service requirement
- Take full account balance with you if you leave the Company

## 6. INVESTMENT OPTIONS

### 6.1 Available Investments

The Plan offers a diverse menu of investment options:

**Target-Date Funds (Default Option):**
- All-in-one diversified portfolios
- Automatically adjust asset allocation as you near retirement
- Available in 5-year increments (e.g., 2030, 2035, 2040)
- Managed by Fidelity

**Equity Funds:**
- U.S. Large-Cap (growth and value)
- U.S. Mid-Cap
- U.S. Small-Cap
- International Developed Markets
- Emerging Markets

**Fixed-Income Funds:**
- Core Bond Fund
- Short-Term Bond Fund
- Inflation-Protected Securities
- High-Yield Bond Fund

**Specialty Funds:**
- Real Estate (REIT)
- Commodities
- Stable Value Fund (capital preservation)

**Self-Directed Brokerage Option:**
- Access to thousands of additional investment options
- Additional fees may apply
- Requires separate election

**Total Investment Options:** 20+ funds covering all major asset classes

### 6.2 Fund Information

Detailed information about each investment option, including:
- Expense ratios
- Historical performance
- Risk ratings
- Portfolio holdings
- Fund fact sheets

Available at www.401k.com or by calling 1-800-555-0401.

### 6.3 Changing Investments

**Future Contributions:**
- Change investment elections for future contributions anytime
- Changes effective next business day

**Existing Balance (Fund Transfers):**
- Transfer existing account balance between investments anytime
- Unlimited transfers (no fees)
- Transfers processed same business day (if submitted by 4pm ET)

**Rebalancing:**
- Automatic rebalancing available (quarterly, semi-annual, or annual)
- Maintains target asset allocation automatically

## 7. PROFESSIONAL INVESTMENT HELP

### 7.1 Managed Accounts

**Fidelity Personalized Planning & Advice:**
- Professional portfolio management based on your goals, risk tolerance, and timeline
- Ongoing monitoring and rebalancing
- Annual fee: 0.40% of managed assets (charged quarterly)
- Enroll online or call Fidelity

### 7.2 Investment Guidance

**Free Resources:**
- Online retirement planning tools
- Investment education library
- Retirement income calculator
- Asset allocation questionnaires

**Fidelity Representatives:**
- Phone guidance: 1-800-555-0401
- One-on-one consultations (schedule through website)
- Educational webinars throughout the year

### 7.3 Financial Planning Tools

Access free tools at www.401k.com:
- Retirement savings calculator
- Social Security estimator
- Budget and expense tracker
- College savings calculator
- Retirement income planner

## 8. ACCESSING YOUR MONEY

### 8.1 While Employed

**In-Service Withdrawals:**

Generally, you cannot withdraw funds while actively employed, except:

**Age 59½ Withdrawals:**
- Available once you reach age 59½
- No penalty or special requirements
- Taxes apply (unless Roth qualified withdrawals)

**Hardship Withdrawals:**
- Available for immediate and heavy financial need
- Limited to employee contributions only (not earnings or match)
- Must demonstrate financial hardship
- Subject to taxes and 10% early withdrawal penalty (if under age 59½)
- See Section 8.3 for qualifying hardships

**Loans:**
- Borrow from your own account
- Must be repaid with interest
- See Section 8.4 for loan provisions

### 8.2 After Separation from Service

**Distribution Options After Leaving Company:**

**Option 1: Leave Money in Plan**
- Keep account in Plan if balance exceeds $7,000
- Continue to manage investments
- No additional contributions allowed
- Take distributions later when needed

**Option 2: Rollover to IRA or New Employer's Plan**
- Roll over to traditional IRA (pre-tax funds)
- Roll over to Roth IRA (Roth funds, or convert pre-tax funds with taxes)
- Roll over to new employer's 401(k) plan (if accepted)
- Maintains tax-deferred/tax-free status
- No taxes or penalties

**Option 3: Cash Distribution**
- Receive lump-sum payment
- Subject to income taxes
- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59½ (with exceptions)
- 20% mandatory federal withholding

**Automatic Cashout:**
- If balance is $1,000-$7,000 and you don't make an election, automatically rolled to IRA
- If balance is under $1,000, automatically cashed out and check mailed

### 8.3 Hardship Withdrawals

**Qualifying Hardship Reasons:**
- Medical expenses for employee, spouse, or dependents
- Costs related to purchase of primary residence (excluding mortgage payments)
- Tuition and educational fees for next 12 months
- Payments to prevent eviction or foreclosure on primary residence
- Burial or funeral expenses for family members
- Expenses to repair damage to primary residence (casualty loss)
- Expenses and losses due to federally declared disaster

**Requirements:**
- Must demonstrate immediate and heavy financial need
- Must have exhausted other resources (loans, insurance, other assets)
- Amount limited to need (plus taxes and penalties)
- Cannot contribute to Plan for 6 months after hardship withdrawal

**Application Process:**
- Complete hardship withdrawal request form
- Provide supporting documentation
- HR and plan administrator review and approve
- Processing time: 7-10 business days

### 8.4 Loans

**Loan Provisions:**
- Borrow minimum of $1,000
- Borrow maximum of lesser of:
  - $50,000 (reduced by highest outstanding loan balance in past 12 months), OR
  - 50% of vested account balance
- Maximum of 2 loans outstanding at any time

**Loan Terms:**
- General purpose loan: Up to 5-year repayment
- Primary residence loan: Up to 15-year repayment
- Interest rate: Prime rate + 1% (fixed at loan origination)
- Interest paid back to your own account

**Repayment:**
- Automatic payroll deduction
- Payments made at least quarterly
- Level amortization (equal payments)

**Fees:**
- Loan origination fee: $75 (one-time)
- Annual maintenance fee: $50 per loan
- Fees deducted from loan proceeds or account balance

**Defaulted Loans:**
- Loan becomes taxable distribution if not repaid
- 10% early withdrawal penalty applies if under age 59½
- Loan defaults if:
  - Payments not made for 90+ days
  - Employment terminates (loan due within 60 days)

**Application:**
- Apply online at www.401k.com or call 1-800-555-0401
- Funds typically available within 5 business days
- Spouse consent required for loans over $5,000

### 8.5 Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs)

**When RMDs Begin:**
- Age 73 (or 75 if born in 1960 or later)
- Must begin by April 1 following year you turn required age
- Exception: Can delay if still employed (and not 5% owner)

**How RMDs Are Calculated:**
- Based on IRS life expectancy tables
- Calculated each year based on prior year-end balance
- Fidelity calculates and notifies you of RMD amount

**Tax Treatment:**
- Pre-tax account RMDs are taxable income
- Roth account RMDs are tax-free (if qualified)
- Failure to take RMD results in 25% penalty on amount not taken

## 9. BENEFICIARY DESIGNATIONS

### 9.1 Importance of Beneficiary Designation

- Designate who receives your account balance if you die
- Beneficiaries avoid probate process
- Can designate primary and contingent beneficiaries
- Can split percentage among multiple beneficiaries

### 9.2 Spousal Rights

**If You Are Married:**
- Spouse is automatic beneficiary unless spouse signs written consent to name someone else
- Spousal consent must be notarized
- Applies even if you designated someone else before marriage

**If You Are Not Married:**
- Can name anyone as beneficiary (person, trust, charity, estate)
- No consent required

### 9.3 Updating Beneficiaries

- Review and update beneficiary designations regularly
- Update after major life events: marriage, divorce, births, deaths
- Update online at www.401k.com or by submitting paper form
- Previous designation revoked by new designation

### 9.4 If No Beneficiary Designated

If no beneficiary is designated or all beneficiaries predecease you:
1. Spouse (if married)
2. Children (equally)
3. Parents (equally)
4. Estate

## 10. PLAN ADMINISTRATION

### 10.1 Plan Year

The Plan operates on a calendar year (January 1 - December 31).

### 10.2 Plan Administrator Responsibilities

**Amazon Inc. serves as Plan Administrator:**
- Interpret and administer Plan provisions
- Make eligibility determinations
- Process distributions and loans
- Ensure compliance with federal regulations
- Coordinate with Fidelity on recordkeeping

### 10.3 Fiduciary Responsibilities

**Plan Fiduciaries:**
- Plan Administrator (Amazon)
- Investment Committee (selects and monitors investment options)
- Trustee (Fidelity Investments)

Fiduciaries act in best interest of Plan participants and beneficiaries.

### 10.4 Plan Costs and Fees

**Plan-Level Administrative Fees:**
- Recordkeeping and administrative costs paid by Company
- No direct charge to participant accounts for plan administration

**Investment Fees:**
- Each fund has an expense ratio (annual management fee)
- Expense ratios range from 0.05% to 1.20% depending on fund
- Deducted from fund returns (not separately billed)

**Individual Transaction Fees:**
- Loan origination: $75
- Loan maintenance: $50 per year per loan
- QDRO processing: $500
- Overnight check delivery: $25
- Paper statement fee: $2 per quarter (waived if opt for electronic)

### 10.5 Plan Amendments and Termination

Amazon reserves the right to:
- Amend the Plan at any time
- Terminate the Plan at any time
- If Plan terminates, all accounts become 100% vested
- Participants will be notified of significant changes

## 11. SPECIAL SITUATIONS

### 11.1 Military Leave

**During Military Service:**
- Can continue contributions if receiving pay
- Employer match continues on contributions made
- Can contribute missed deferrals when returning (within specified timeframe)

**Returning from Military Service:**
- Treated as continuously employed for vesting purposes
- Eligible for makeup contributions based on military pay

### 11.2 Leave of Absence

**During Approved Unpaid Leave:**
- Cannot make contributions (no compensation)
- No employer match while not receiving pay
- Account remains invested
- Can make investment changes
- Vesting service may continue depending on leave type (FMLA, military)

### 11.3 Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (QDRO)

If you divorce, a court may order that part of your account be paid to your former spouse:
- Order must be a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO)
- Company reviews order to ensure it meets Plan and legal requirements
- Once approved, account divided according to order
- Former spouse (alternate payee) can roll over or take distribution

**QDRO Review Fee:** $500 (charged to participant account)

### 11.4 Disability

If you become disabled and receive long-term disability benefits:
- Can continue contributions if receiving disability pay from Company
- If disability pay ceases, contributions stop
- Account remains invested
- May access funds if disabled (before age 59½) without 10% penalty

### 11.5 Death

**Upon Participant Death:**
- Beneficiaries notified and sent claim forms
- Account balance paid to designated beneficiaries
- Spousal beneficiaries have multiple options (keep in plan, rollover, distribute)
- Non-spousal beneficiaries must take distributions (but can stretch over lifetime)

## 12. IMPORTANT LEGAL INFORMATION

### 12.1 Plan Documents

This policy summary provides an overview of the Plan. The official Plan Document and Summary Plan Description (SPD) govern in case of any discrepancy.

Documents available:
- Summary Plan Description (SPD)
- Plan Document
- Trust Agreement
- Annual notices

Access documents at: www.401k.com or request printed copy from HR.

### 12.2 ERISA Rights

As a participant in the Plan, you are entitled to certain rights and protections under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA):

**Receive Information About Your Plan:**
- Examine Plan documents at HR office
- Obtain copies of Plan documents (reasonable fee may apply)
- Receive Summary Annual Report (SAR) each year

**Prudent Actions by Plan Fiduciaries:**
- Plan fiduciaries must act prudently and in interest of participants
- If fiduciaries misuse Plan assets, they can be held responsible

**Enforce Your Rights:**
- If claim is denied, right to appeal
- Right to sue for benefits
- Right to protection from retaliation for exercising ERISA rights

### 12.3 Claims and Appeals

**If Distribution Claim Is Denied:**
- Receive written explanation within 90 days
- Appeal denial within 60 days
- Decision on appeal within 60 days
- If appeal denied, may file suit in federal court

## 13. TAX INFORMATION

### 13.1 Tax Treatment of Contributions

**Pre-Tax Contributions:**
- Reduce taxable income in year contributed
- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code D
- Reduce federal income tax (and usually state)
- Do not reduce Social Security or Medicare tax

**Roth Contributions:**
- Made with after-tax dollars
- Do not reduce taxable income
- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code AA

### 13.2 Tax Treatment of Distributions

**Pre-Tax Account Distributions:**
- Fully taxable as ordinary income
- Added to taxable income in year received
- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59½ (with exceptions)

**Roth Account Distributions:**
- Contributions always tax-free
- Earnings tax-free if qualified distribution (age 59½ + 5-year rule)
- Earnings taxable if non-qualified distribution
- 10% penalty applies to taxable portion if under age 59½

**Withholding:**
- 20% mandatory federal withholding on eligible rollover distributions paid to you
- Can avoid withholding by choosing direct rollover
- State withholding may also apply

### 13.3 Form 1099-R

- Fidelity issues Form 1099-R by January 31 for prior year distributions
- Reports taxable distributions to IRS
- Use to prepare tax return
- Keep for your records

### 13.4 Consult a Tax Advisor

This summary provides general tax information. Individual tax situations vary. Consult a qualified tax advisor for advice specific to your situation.

## What Can and Cannot Be Seeded

### CAN Be Seeded (Pre-populated Before Task Starts)
| Type | How | Notes |
|------|-----|-------|
| **Emails** | `seed_emails` in task JSON | Appears in agent's inbox |
| **Calendar Events** | `seed_calendar_events` in task JSON | Events on NPC calendars |
| **Chat Channels** | `seed_group_channels` in task JSON | Pre-existing channel membership + history context |
| **NPC Secrets** | `npcs[].secret` in task JSON | Info NPCs reveal when asked via Chat |
| **HRIS Data** | Frappe seed files | Employee records, candidates, requisitions |

### CANNOT Be Seeded
| Type | Why | Alternative |
|------|-----|-------------|
| **DM History** | Direct-message threads cannot be pre-seeded | Use `seed_emails`, NPC secrets, or seeded channels |

### Task Design Implications

**Tasks must NOT assume:**
- Existing DM history the agent can read at task start
- Ability for the agent to create channels/groups

**Tasks MAY assume (when seeded):**
- Existing channel context/messages in `seed_group_channels`
- Use of explicitly named channels already present in seed data

**If task needs info from someone:**
1. Use `seed_emails` (email from the NPC forwarding relevant info)
2. Add an NPC `secret` (agent asks via Chat, NPC replies)
3. Include directly in task description

### Multi-Person Decisions

For panel interviews, debriefs, or group decisions:
- **Each decision-maker should have their own NPC secret**
- Secrets should reflect individual perspectives/feedback
- This ensures agent must consult each relevant stakeholder

Example for 3-person interview panel:
```json
{
  "npcs": [
    {"id": "michael_chen", "secret": "I thought the candidate was strong on system design but needs more depth on algorithms."},
    {"id": "emily_davis", "secret": "Good culture fit, but I have concerns about their experience with our scale."},
    {"id": "sarah_johnson", "secret": "I'd recommend a hire - their leadership experience outweighs the technical gaps."}
  ]
}
```

"}, {"name": "candidate_faq", "title": "Compensation & Equity", "content": "# Candidate FAQ

## Compensation & Equity

### How is base salary determined?
Base salary is set based on role, level, location, and experience. Each level has a defined range (min/mid/max). Most offers start at or near the midpoint.

### How do RSUs work?
RSUs vest over 4 years with a 1-year cliff:
- Year 1: 5% (at 12-month anniversary)
- Year 2: 15% (quarterly vesting)
- Years 3-4: 40% each year (quarterly vesting)

RSUs are taxed as ordinary income when they vest.

### Is the sign-on bonus negotiable?
Sign-on bonuses are used to bridge the gap when candidates have unvested equity at their current company. We can discuss your specific situation.

### What about annual raises and bonuses?
- Annual merit increases: typically 3-8% based on performance
- Annual bonus target varies by level, paid in Q1
- Equity refresh grants: awarded annually based on performance

## Benefits

### When do benefits start?
Benefits effective dates vary by program. Some benefits may begin on your first day, while others may have eligibility requirements or waiting periods (see the relevant plan documents).

### What health insurance options are available?
We offer medical coverage options. For the authoritative plan details (deductibles, copays, out-of-pocket max, network rules), see `health_insurance_plan.md`.

### Is the PTO really unlimited?
Yes, we have an unlimited discretionary PTO policy. We encourage at least 2 weeks per year. Manager approval is required but rarely denied for reasonable requests.

### What's the 401(k) match?
See `401K_policy.md` for eligibility, match formula, and vesting details.

## Work Arrangements

### What does hybrid mean?
Our hybrid model requires 3 days per week in the office (typically Tue-Thu). 2 days can be worked remotely. Core collaboration hours are 10am-3pm local time.

### Can I work fully remote?
Fully remote arrangements are considered case-by-case and depend on role requirements. Discuss with your hiring manager if this is important to you.

### What about relocation?
- **Domestic moves**: $10,000 lump sum
- **International moves**: $25,000 lump sum + visa sponsorship
- **Temporary housing**: 30 days covered for domestic moves

## Timeline & Process

### When will I hear back after my interview?
We aim to make decisions within 5 business days of your final interview. Your recruiter will reach out with next steps.

### How long is the offer valid?
Offers typically expire within 3-5 business days. If you need more time, please discuss with your recruiter.

### What happens after I accept?
1. Background check initiated (typically 3-5 business days)
2. I-9 verification and new hire paperwork
3. Laptop and equipment shipped before start date
4. Welcome email with Day 1 logistics

### Can I negotiate my start date?
Yes, start dates are flexible within reason. Most candidates start on a Monday. We typically can accommodate 2-4 weeks from offer acceptance.

## Team & Role

### What's the onboarding like?
- Week 1: Orientation, compliance training, team introductions
- Weeks 2-4: Role-specific training, 1:1s with stakeholders
- First 90 days: Defined onboarding projects with clear success metrics

## Visa & Work Authorization

### Do you sponsor visas?
Yes, we sponsor H-1B, L-1, and green cards for eligible roles. Discuss your specific situation with your recruiter.

### I'm on OPT/STEM OPT—is that okay?
Yes, we can hire candidates on OPT or STEM OPT and sponsor H-1B before the authorization expires.

"}, {"name": "disciplinary_action_policy", "title": "DISCIPLINARY ACTION POLICY", "content": "# WEAVER ENTERPRISES INC.
## DISCIPLINARY ACTION POLICY

**Document Number:** HR-POL-009  
**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  
**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  
**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  
**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer

---

## 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE

This policy establishes guidelines for addressing employee misconduct and policy violations at Weaver Enterprises Inc. The purpose is to:

- Correct unacceptable behavior and performance issues
- Ensure fair and consistent treatment of all employees
- Maintain a productive and professional work environment
- Comply with employment laws and regulations
- Provide employees with clear expectations and opportunities to improve
- Protect the organization and other employees

This policy applies to all employees. The Company reserves the right to exercise discretion in determining appropriate disciplinary action based on the specific circumstances of each situation.

## 2. EMPLOYMENT AT-WILL

Employment at Weaver Enterprises is at-will, meaning either the employee or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause or notice.

**This policy does not:**
- Create a contract of employment
- Guarantee employment for any specific period
- Require progressive discipline before termination
- Limit the Company's right to terminate employment

Progressive discipline is generally followed when appropriate, but the Company reserves the right to skip steps or proceed directly to termination for serious violations.

## 3. GENERAL PRINCIPLES

### 3.1 Consistency and Fairness

- Similar violations should result in similar consequences
- Discipline should be proportionate to the severity of the offense
- Individual circumstances may warrant different approaches
- Documentation is essential for all disciplinary actions

### 3.2 Corrective Purpose

The goal of discipline is correction, not punishment:
- Help employees understand expectations
- Provide opportunity to improve
- Support employees in meeting standards
- Separate employment only when necessary

### 3.3 Confidentiality

- Disciplinary matters kept confidential
- Information shared only on need-to-know basis
- Employee privacy respected
- Documentation stored securely in personnel files

### 3.4 Non-Retaliation

Employees will not face retaliation for:
- Reporting policy violations in good faith
- Participating in investigations
- Exercising legal rights
- Engaging in protected activities

Retaliation is itself a serious policy violation subject to discipline.

## 4. PROGRESSIVE DISCIPLINE FRAMEWORK

### 4.1 Progressive Discipline Steps

When appropriate, the Company follows progressive discipline:

**Step 1: Verbal Warning**
- Informal discussion of concern
- Explanation of expected behavior
- Documentation in manager's notes
- No formal documentation in personnel file

**Step 2: Written Warning**
- Formal written documentation
- Specific description of violation
- Expected corrective action
- Timeline for improvement
- Consequences of continued violations
- Placed in personnel file

**Step 3: Final Written Warning**
- Formal documentation of continued or serious violation
- Clear statement that employment is in jeopardy
- Specific expectations and timeline
- Notification that next violation may result in termination
- Placed in personnel file

**Step 4: Suspension (with or without pay)**
- Employee removed from workplace temporarily
- Allows for investigation or cooling-off period
- May be paid or unpaid depending on circumstances
- Duration typically 1-5 days
- Return-to-work conditions specified

**Step 5: Termination**
- Employment relationship ended
- Final step in progressive discipline
- Or immediate action for serious violations

### 4.2 When Progressive Discipline Applies

Progressive discipline is generally appropriate for:
- Attendance and punctuality issues
- Minor policy violations
- Work quality or productivity concerns (when not addressed through performance management)
- First-time minor infractions
- Situations where employee can reasonably improve

### 4.3 When Progressive Discipline May Be Skipped

The Company may bypass progressive discipline and proceed directly to suspension or termination for:
- Serious misconduct or policy violations
- Illegal activity
- Behavior that threatens safety or security
- Gross insubordination
- Theft, fraud, or dishonesty
- Violence or threats of violence
- Harassment or discrimination
- Breach of confidentiality
- Intoxication or drug use at work
- Willful destruction of Company property
- Conduct causing significant harm to Company or employees

## 5. COMMON VIOLATIONS AND POTENTIAL CONSEQUENCES

### 5.1 Attendance and Punctuality

**Examples:**
- Excessive absenteeism
- Chronic tardiness
- Leaving work early without permission
- No-call, no-show
- Abuse of break or lunch periods

**Typical Discipline:**
- 1st occurrence: Verbal warning
- 2nd occurrence: Written warning
- 3rd occurrence: Final written warning
- 4th occurrence: Termination

**Exception:** No-call, no-show for 3 consecutive days may result in immediate termination for job abandonment.

### 5.2 Performance Issues

**Examples:**
- Failure to meet job requirements
- Substandard work quality
- Lack of productivity
- Failure to follow instructions

**Note:** Performance issues are generally addressed through performance management process (see Performance Management Policy), not disciplinary action. Discipline appropriate when performance issues involve:
- Willful failure to perform duties
- Refusal to follow reasonable instructions
- Negligent or careless work causing harm

### 5.3 Policy Violations

**Examples:**
- Dress code violations
- Inappropriate use of technology
- Smoking in prohibited areas
- Failure to follow safety procedures
- Unauthorized overtime

**Typical Discipline:**
- 1st occurrence: Verbal or written warning
- 2nd occurrence: Written or final written warning
- 3rd occurrence: Suspension or termination

Severity of consequence depends on nature and impact of violation.

### 5.4 Insubordination

**Examples:**
- Refusal to follow reasonable work directive
- Disrespectful behavior toward supervisor
- Undermining manager's authority
- Refusing assigned tasks without legitimate reason

**Typical Discipline:**
- Minor insubordination: Written warning
- Serious insubordination: Final written warning or suspension
- Gross insubordination: Immediate termination

### 5.5 Conduct and Behavior

**Examples:**
- Unprofessional behavior
- Rudeness to coworkers, customers, or vendors
- Gossip or spreading rumors
- Inappropriate language
- Creating hostile work environment

**Typical Discipline:**
- 1st occurrence: Written warning
- 2nd occurrence: Final written warning or suspension
- 3rd occurrence: Termination

Harassment, discrimination, or bullying may result in immediate termination (see Section 5.7).

### 5.6 Dishonesty and Integrity

**Examples:**
- Falsifying records or documents
- Lying to supervisor or during investigation
- Time theft (falsifying time records)
- Providing false information on application or resume
- Theft of Company or employee property
- Fraud or embezzlement
- Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information

**Typical Discipline:**
- Immediate termination for most violations
- Investigation and suspension pending investigation
- May involve law enforcement referral

### 5.7 Harassment, Discrimination, and Retaliation

**Examples:**
- Sexual harassment
- Harassment based on protected characteristics
- Discrimination in any form
- Retaliation against someone who reports concerns
- Creating hostile work environment
- Bullying or intimidating behavior

**Typical Discipline:**
- Immediate suspension pending investigation
- Termination if substantiated
- Mandatory training if less severe (rare)

Zero tolerance for harassment, discrimination, and retaliation.

### 5.8 Safety Violations

**Examples:**
- Failure to follow safety procedures
- Not wearing required personal protective equipment
- Creating unsafe conditions
- Failure to report accidents or injuries
- Operating equipment without authorization
- Horseplay or reckless behavior

**Typical Discipline:**
- Minor safety violation: Written warning
- Serious safety violation: Final written warning or suspension
- Egregious safety violation: Immediate termination

### 5.9 Substance Abuse

**Examples:**
- Reporting to work under the influence of alcohol or drugs
- Using, possessing, or distributing illegal drugs at work
- Alcohol consumption during work hours (except approved events)
- Refusing drug/alcohol test when required

**Typical Discipline:**
- Immediate suspension pending investigation
- Termination for confirmed violation
- May be offered EAP assistance in some circumstances

### 5.10 Technology and Social Media

**Examples:**
- Accessing inappropriate websites
- Excessive personal use of company technology
- Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information online
- Posting disparaging comments about Company or employees
- Using Company equipment for personal business
- Installing unauthorized software

**Typical Discipline:**
- Minor violation: Written warning
- Serious violation: Final written warning or termination
- Breach of confidentiality: Immediate termination

### 5.11 Violence and Threats

**Examples:**
- Physical violence or assault
- Threats of violence
- Intimidating or threatening behavior
- Possession of weapons on Company property (except where legally authorized)
- Stalking or menacing behavior

**Typical Discipline:**
- Immediate suspension or termination
- Law enforcement involvement
- Workplace violence threat assessment
- No tolerance policy

## 6. DISCIPLINARY PROCESS

### 6.1 Investigation

Before imposing discipline (except verbal warnings):
- Gather facts about the alleged violation
- Interview relevant parties and witnesses
- Review applicable policies and documentation
- Consider employee's explanation and circumstances
- Consult with HR

**Investigation principles:**
- Prompt and thorough
- Fair and impartial
- Confidential to extent possible
- Well-documented

### 6.2 Deciding Appropriate Action

Consider the following factors:
- Severity of the violation
- Impact on Company, employees, or customers
- Employee's disciplinary history
- Length of service and overall performance
- Mitigating or aggravating circumstances
- Consistency with past practice
- Whether employee knew or should have known rule
- Intent (willful vs. negligent vs. accidental)

### 6.3 Disciplinary Meeting

**Preparation:**
- Review all facts and documentation
- Determine appropriate disciplinary action
- Prepare disciplinary documentation
- Consult with HR
- Schedule private meeting

**Meeting Structure:**
1. Explain purpose of meeting
2. Describe the violation with specific facts
3. Allow employee to respond and provide explanation
4. Explain disciplinary action and consequences
5. Outline expectations going forward
6. Review written documentation with employee
7. Answer questions
8. Have employee sign acknowledgment

**Meeting Best Practices:**
- Remain calm and professional
- Focus on facts and behavior, not personality
- Listen to employee's perspective
- Don't argue or become defensive
- Be clear and direct
- End meeting on constructive note when possible
- Have HR or witness present for serious discipline

### 6.4 Documentation Requirements

**Disciplinary documentation must include:**
- Employee name and position
- Date of violation and date of disciplinary action
- Specific description of violation (who, what, when, where)
- Which policy or rule was violated
- Employee's explanation or response
- Prior disciplinary actions (if any)
- Disciplinary action taken
- Expected corrective action
- Consequences of future violations
- Signatures (employee and manager)

**Employee Signature:**
- Signature indicates receipt, not agreement
- Employee may add written comments
- If employee refuses to sign, note refusal and have witness sign

**Document Distribution:**
- Original to personnel file
- Copy to employee
- Copy to manager's file
- Copy to HR

### 6.5 Suspension Pending Investigation

**When Used:**
- Serious allegations requiring investigation
- Safety concern or risk to others
- Potential criminal activity
- Need to preserve evidence
- Employee's presence would interfere with investigation

**Process:**
- Typically paid suspension (unless policy violation merits unpaid)
- Employee notified verbally and in writing
- Expected duration communicated
- Investigation completed promptly
- Employee notified of outcome

**Possible Outcomes:**
- Reinstatement with no discipline (if unfounded)
- Reinstatement with discipline (if substantiated)
- Termination (if violation warrants)

## 7. TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT

### 7.1 Voluntary Termination

**Resignation:**
- Employee provides notice (2 weeks standard, more for senior roles)
- Submit written resignation letter
- Complete exit process
- Return Company property
- Final paycheck includes accrued PTO (if eligible per policy)

**Job Abandonment:**
- No-call, no-show for 3 consecutive days
- Considered voluntary resignation
- Employment terminated
- Documentation in file

### 7.2 Involuntary Termination

**Termination for Cause:**
- Due to misconduct or policy violation
- May follow progressive discipline or be immediate
- Final paycheck includes earned wages only (no PTO payout in most cases)
- Not eligible for rehire

**Termination for Performance:**
- Failure to meet performance standards
- Typically follows Performance Improvement Plan
- May include PTO payout per policy
- Rehire eligibility determined case-by-case

**Layoff/Reduction in Force:**
- Business-driven (not performance or conduct)
- May include severance if eligible
- Includes PTO payout per policy
- Eligible for rehire

### 7.3 Termination Process

**Manager Responsibilities:**
1. Consult with HR before termination decision
2. Review documentation supporting termination
3. Prepare termination letter
4. Schedule termination meeting (recommend day other than Friday)
5. Arrange for witness (HR representative)
6. Plan logistics (return of property, system access, final pay)

**Termination Meeting:**
- Keep meeting brief (10-15 minutes)
- State decision clearly and directly
- Provide brief reason (already documented)
- Do not argue or negotiate
- Explain final paycheck and benefits
- Collect Company property (keys, badge, laptop, phone)
- Outline next steps
- Escort from building if necessary

**What to Say:**
"We've asked you to meet with us today to inform you that your employment with Weaver Enterprises is ending, effective today. This decision is based on [brief reason - e.g., the attendance issues we discussed in your prior written warnings]. This decision is final. [HR Representative] will explain your final paycheck, benefits continuation, and return of Company property."

**What Not to Say:**
- Don't apologize (undermines decision)
- Don't go into extensive detail or debate
- Don't compare to other employees
- Don't make promises about references
- Don't provide advice on unemployment benefits

### 7.4 Final Pay and Benefits

**Final Paycheck:**
- Due on last day (or next regular payday, per state law)
- Includes all earned wages through termination date
- PTO payout per Leave Policy
- Deductions for unreturned property allowed in some states

**Benefits Continuation:**
- Health insurance ends last day of month of termination
- COBRA notice mailed within 14 days
- 401(k) account remains (employee can rollover or maintain)
- Life insurance and other benefits end per plan terms

**Return of Property:**
- Laptop, phone, tablet
- Keys, access cards, badges
- Credit cards
- Company files and documents
- Any other Company property

## 8. EMPLOYEE RIGHTS AND RESPONSIBILITIES

### 8.1 Right to Respond

Employees have the right to:
- Provide their side of the story
- Present relevant information or evidence
- Explain circumstances or context
- Add written comments to disciplinary documentation
- Request HR involvement

### 8.2 Right to Representation

- Employees may request HR presence at disciplinary meetings
- Union employees may request union representative (if applicable)
- No right to attorney at Company disciplinary meetings

### 8.3 Appeal Process

Employees may appeal disciplinary actions:

**Who Can Appeal:**
- Any employee receiving written warning or higher
- Within 5 business days of disciplinary action

**How to Appeal:**
- Submit written appeal to HR
- State reason for appeal and supporting information
- Provide any additional evidence

**Appeal Review:**
- HR and senior leader review appeal
- May interview parties again
- Decision within 10 business days
- Appeal decision is final

**Possible Outcomes:**
- Uphold discipline
- Reduce discipline
- Remove discipline
- Request additional investigation

### 8.4 Record Retention

**Active Warnings:**
- Verbal warnings: Not in official file (manager notes only)
- Written warnings: Remain in file for 12 months
- Final written warnings: Remain in file for 24 months
- Suspensions: Remain in file permanently
- Terminations: Remain in file permanently

**Removal of Warnings:**
- After expiration period, may be removed from "active" file
- Moved to separate file but retained per retention policy
- Still considered if future violations occur during active period

## 9. MANAGER RESPONSIBILITIES

### 9.1 Addressing Issues Promptly

Managers should:
- Address issues as they arise
- Not ignore or delay discipline
- Provide real-time feedback
- Document performance and conduct concerns
- Escalate to HR when appropriate

### 9.2 Consistency and Fairness

Managers must:
- Apply policies consistently
- Treat similar situations similarly
- Avoid favoritism or bias
- Document all disciplinary actions
- Consult HR for guidance

### 9.3 Required HR Involvement

Managers must consult HR before:
- Issuing final written warning
- Suspending employee
- Terminating employee
- Addressing potential harassment or discrimination
- Situations involving legal risk

HR approval required for:
- All terminations
- All suspensions
- Final written warnings

## 10. HR RESPONSIBILITIES

HR is responsible for:
- Providing guidance on disciplinary process
- Ensuring legal compliance
- Reviewing disciplinary documentation
- Participating in serious disciplinary meetings
- Conducting or overseeing investigations
- Maintaining disciplinary records
- Training managers on disciplinary procedures
- Monitoring for consistency across organization
- Handling appeals

## 11. TRAINING AND RESOURCES

### 11.1 Manager Training

**Required Training:**
- Disciplinary action procedures (3 hours)
- Conducting difficult conversations (2 hours)
- Legal compliance in discipline (2 hours)
- Documenting performance and conduct (2 hours)

**Ongoing Support:**
- HR consultation on specific situations
- Coaching on disciplinary conversations
- Review of documentation before meetings

### 11.2 Available Resources

**Templates and Tools:**
- Disciplinary action forms
- Termination checklists
- Documentation guidelines
- Sample language for common violations
